sections.narrative: Access control is TrustDB's strongest differentiator, backed by 116 CBAC tests, 110 RDKC tests, 23 EACB tests, and 168 adversarial gym test functions. CBAC implements a 6-state governance circuit breaker (Open, Restricted, Suspended, Quarantined, Sealed, EngagementAuthorized) with compare-and-swap semantics. REWG derives entitlements from graph position via bitwise AND — no ACL tables, no policy engine. EACB provides FROST threshold signatures for LAWS 3000.09 human-in-the-loop authorization. Most critically, access control is cryptographic: RDKC derives unique per-page keys from governance state (verified by gym_c02 with 31 correctness tests including avalanche, determinism, and collision detection). A CUI-clearance entity cannot derive the key for a SECRET page — this is verified by gym_g01 clearance boundary battery (6 named attack scenarios) and gym_p01 pre-redaction leakage proof (7 memory-level tests). The gap to 9.5: FIPS ACVP formal validation, timing side-channel proof (gym_s01 exists with 7 tests but needs target-hardware validation), and ML-DSA-87 post-quantum signatures on CBAC audit entries.

history.note: CBAC 6-state (PRE-04), RDKC+CBAC wired (B01), EACB/FROST (B02), REWG wired (PRE-03). 116 CBAC tests, 110 RDKC tests, 168 adversarial gym functions. Up from 2.0.

benchmarks.name: WAL Tamper Protection
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total. Per-record WalProvenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp). ACVP test vectors (RFC 4231)
benchmarks.source: PR #247. Chained HMAC: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data). Tamper any record → invalidates entire downstream chain. WalProvenance: 26 bytes per record (NIST AU-10 non-repudiation). Static or RDKC-derived key modes. CNSA 2.0 compliant. ACVP vectors for NIST CAVP submission.

comparisons.source: Corruption detection only — no chain, no provenance

sections.narrative: Encryption is TrustDB's defining capability, backed by 110 RDKC tests, 41 CAM tests, 10 page_crypto tests, 9 transport tests, 25 rekey tests, and 89 adversarial gym test functions across 10 gym files. Every 16KB page is encrypted with AES-256-GCM-SIV (nonce-misuse resistant) using a unique key derived by RDKC from four governance inputs: CBAC state, GEMVT clearance, REWG entitlements, and temporal window. This is verified by gym_e02 (10 input independence tests proving each governance dimension is a live cryptographic dependency) and gym_c02 (31 correctness tests: determinism across 10K runs, avalanche effect, 1M collision search). Structural key expiry is verified by gym_e01 (12 tests proving keys become mathematically underivable when governance state changes — not revoked, not expired, impossible to derive). Post-quantum key transport (ML-KEM-1024 + X25519 hybrid, 9 tests) exceeds every competitor. WAF=3.03 invariance proves the entire stack adds zero additional I/O. Gap to 9.5: FIPS 140-3 formal submission (ACVP vectors exist in gym_c01 but haven't been submitted to NIST). WAL HMAC chain now complete (PR #247 — chained HMAC-SHA384 with per-record WalProvenance, 33 WAL tests).

history.note: Full per-page encryption: RDKC (A01, 110 tests), AES-256-GCM-SIV (B01, 10 page_crypto tests), ML-KEM-1024 transport (B03, 9 tests), CNSA 2.0 profiles (A06, 41 CAM tests), re-encryption (A07, 25 tests), 89 adversarial gym functions. Gaps: FIPS formal submission, WAL HMAC. Up from 3.0.

benchmarks.name: WAL HMAC Chain (Tamper-Proof Audit)
benchmarks.value: HMAC-SHA384 chained format (PR #247) — 33 WAL tests total (9 HMAC-specific, 14 WAL integration, 8 epoch WAL, 2 partitioned WAL)
benchmarks.source: PR #247. Chained HMAC format v2: [0xCB magic][provenance:26B][record:N][hmac_tag:48B] = 75 bytes overhead per record. WalProvenance per record (26 bytes): agent_id (16B AgentId), clearance (1B CBAC level), connectivity (1B Online/Offline/Unknown), write_ts_ms (8B wall-clock). Chain computation: each tag = HMAC(key, prev_tag || provenance || data) — tampering any record invalidates all subsequent records (non-repudiation). Key modes: (1) static key for accidental corruption detection, (2) RDKC-derived key from governance context for NIST AU-10 classified deployments. ACVP test vectors (RFC 4231 TC2/TC3) for NIST CAVP validation prep. Backward compatible: supports v2 chained + v1 HMAC + legacy FNV recovery paths. CNSA 2.0 compliant.

comparisons.source: Detects accidental corruption only — not adversarial tampering
Same limitation — no chain, no provenance
WiredTiger journal has no checksums exposed

benchmarks.name: SSP Evidence Appendix (NIST SP 800-53)
benchmarks.value: GYM-R01 — automated control mapping from test evidence to NIST controls
benchmarks.source: GYM-R01 merged to main. System Security Plan evidence appendix auto-generated from gym test results. Maps TrustDB security tests to NIST SP 800-53 controls (AC, AU, SC, IA families). Verifiable compliance evidence from actual test execution, not self-certification.

benchmarks.name: Compliance Export (SOX/HIPAA/NIST)
benchmarks.value: ComplianceReport with 7 control attestations (PR #250). SOX 404, HIPAA 164.312(b)/(c)(1), NIST AU-2/3/10/12. Time-window filtering, JSON serialization
benchmarks.source: PR #250. ComplianceReport generates structured compliance evidence from WAL HMAC chain and provenance data. 7 control attestations: SOX Section 404 (ICFR), HIPAA 164.312(b) (Audit Controls), HIPAA 164.312(c)(1) (Integrity), NIST AU-2 (Auditable Events), NIST AU-3 (Content of Audit Records), NIST AU-10 (Non-repudiation), NIST AU-12 (Audit Generation). Time-window filtering for period-based compliance reporting. JSON serialization for automated compliance tooling.

comparisons.source: Manual mapping to compliance controls required
Structured evidence from WAL provenance chain

benchmarks.name: SIEM Integration (CEF/STIX)
benchmarks.value: CEF for ArcSight/Splunk + STIX 2.1 bundles for Sentinel/Elastic (PR #250). 4 event types with severity mapping
benchmarks.source: PR #250. CEF (Common Event Format): ArcSight/Splunk compatible event export with severity mapping (1-10 scale). STIX 2.1 bundles: Sentinel/Elastic compatible threat intelligence format. 4 event types: governance state change, access control violation, WAL integrity alert, provenance chain break. Each event carries full WalProvenance context (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp).

sections.narrative: Auditing is now a flagship capability (9.5) with all previously identified gaps closed. WAL HMAC chain (PR #247): chained HMAC-SHA384 format where each record's tag covers the previous tag plus WalProvenance (agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp), creating a cryptographic chain of custody. 33 WAL tests across 4 modules. Per-record provenance at 26 bytes delivers NIST AU-10 (Non-repudiation) compliance. Compliance export (PR #250): ComplianceReport generates structured evidence mapped to 7 control attestations (SOX 404, HIPAA 164.312(b)/(c)(1), NIST AU-2/3/10/12) with time-window filtering. SIEM integration (PR #250): CEF for ArcSight/Splunk and STIX 2.1 bundles for Sentinel/Elastic, with 4 event types and severity mapping. GYM-R01 SSP Evidence Appendix auto-generates NIST SP 800-53 mappings from gym test results. ProvenanceChain tracks who/what/when at zero overhead (inline with LeafEntry). TCI discovers implicit relationships at flush time. CBAC state transition logging records every governance change (116 tests + 20 adversarial). 42 total security/audit tests. TrustDB is now the only embedded database with cryptographic per-record WAL provenance, automated compliance reporting, and native SIEM export in both CEF and STIX formats.

history.note: CBAC audit trail added. 48 adversarial gym functions. Gaps: WAL HMAC, compliance export, SIEM.
PRE-05 WAL HMAC-SHA384 (7 tests) + GYM-R01 SSP Evidence Appendix (NIST SP 800-53 mapping). WAL tamper-proof.

PR #247 WAL HMAC chain: chained format (each tag covers prev_tag + provenance + data), WalProvenance per record (26B: agent_id, clearance, connectivity, timestamp), RDKC-derived key mode, ACVP vectors. 33 WAL tests total.

PR #250: ComplianceReport (7 control attestations: SOX 404, HIPAA, NIST AU-2/3/10/12) + SIEM integration (CEF for ArcSight/Splunk, STIX 2.1 for Sentinel/Elastic, 4 event types). All auditing gaps closed. 42 total tests.
